title: A Prayer for the Beginning of Lent
text:
Lord our God,
you invite us to turn away from sin and to fix our hearts on you.

In this holy season of Lent, grant us the grace of genuine repentance — not merely the outward gesture of fasting, but the inward transformation of a contrite heart.

May we use these forty days wisely: to pray with greater fervour, to fast with discipline and love, and to give generously to those who lack what we take for granted.

Draw us closer to the Cross, that in dying to ourselves we may rise with your Son, Jesus Christ, who lives and reigns with you and the Holy Spirit, one God, for ever and ever.

Amen.
scripture: Return to me with your whole heart, with fasting, weeping, and mourning. Rend your hearts, not your garments. — Joel 2:12–13
reflection: What is one thing I can let go of this Lent to make more room for God?
source: Ash Wednesday Collect, Roman Missal (3rd Edition)

nextActions.label: Donate to the Lent Appeal
nextActions.description: Help CAFOD reach families facing hunger and poverty

nextActions.label: Join the Big Lent Walk
nextActions.description: Walk, fundraise, and fast together this Lent

title: For Those Who Suffer
text:
Gracious God, during this season of Lent we bring before you all who carry heavy burdens — those living with illness, those fleeing violence, those who hunger while we are fed.

Forgive us for the times we have looked away. Open our eyes to the face of Christ in the suffering stranger.

May our fasting make us hungry enough to notice those who cannot choose when they eat. May our giving be more than what we can easily spare. May our prayer be not only words, but a changed life.

We ask this through Jesus Christ, who took on our poverty that we might share in his richness.

Amen.
scripture: Whatever you did for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you did for me. — Matthew 25:40
reflection: Who in my life is carrying a burden I have not yet noticed?
attribution: Inspired by CAFOD Lenten Reflection Series

nextActions.label: Donate to the Lent Appeal
nextActions.description: Turn your prayer into action for those who hunger

nextActions.label: Sign the petition
nextActions.description: Call on world leaders to end hunger for good

title: Lord, Make Me an Instrument
scripture: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good. — Romans 12:21
reflection: Where is God calling me to be a peacemaker today?

nextActions.label: CAFOD newsletter
nextActions.description: Stories of peace and hope from around the world

title: A Prayer for True Fasting
text:
Lord God, you call us to fast —
not merely from food, but from indifference.

Teach us the fast that you desire:
to loose the chains of injustice,
to untie the cords of the yoke,
to set the oppressed free
and break every burden.

May our hunger remind us of those
who have no choice but to go without.
May our emptiness make room
for your Spirit to work in us.

We ask this through Christ our Lord.

Amen.
scripture: Is not this the kind of fasting I have chosen: to loose the chains of injustice and untie the cords of the yoke, to set the oppressed free and break every yoke? — Isaiah 58:6
reflection: Beyond food, what habit or attitude is God inviting me to fast from?

nextActions.label: Donate to the Lent Appeal
nextActions.description: Help CAFOD reach families facing hunger

title: For Clean Water
text:
Creator God, you made water the source of all life —
in the beginning, your Spirit moved over the waters;
in baptism, you make us new.

Today we pray for the millions who walk miles
for water that may make them sick.
We pray for mothers who watch their children thirst.
We pray for communities whose rivers have dried
or whose wells have been poisoned.

Give us the will to act,
the generosity to share,
and the courage to demand justice
for every person denied this most basic gift.

Through Christ, the living water.

Amen.
scripture: Let justice roll on like a river, righteousness like a never-failing stream! — Amos 5:24
reflection: How can I use the resources I have more justly today?
source: Inspired by CAFOD Clean Water Appeal

title: A Prayer for Mercy
text:
Lord Jesus Christ, Son of the living God,
have mercy on me, a sinner.

In this season of repentance, I come to you
not with the confidence of the righteous
but with the humility of the tax collector
who could not even lift his eyes to heaven.

You know my weakness.
You know every time I have chosen comfort over compassion,
silence over solidarity,
fear over faith.

Meet me here in my poverty,
and by your mercy, make me rich
in love, in hope, and in courage.

Amen.
scripture: The tax collector stood at a distance. He would not even look up to heaven, but beat his breast and said, 'God, have mercy on me, a sinner.' — Luke 18:13
reflection: Where do I most need God's mercy in my life right now?
attribution: Based on the Jesus Prayer and Luke 18

title: Prayer in the Desert
text:
God of the wilderness,
you led your people through the desert
and you led your Son into forty days of testing.

Lead me now into the desert of Lent —
not to punish, but to prepare;
not to deprive, but to reveal
what I truly need.

Strip away the noise that drowns your voice.
Strip away the comfort that dulls my heart.
Leave me with nothing but your presence,
and let that be enough.

Amen.
scripture: Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry. — Matthew 4:1–2
reflection: What noise in my life might God be asking me to turn down this Lent?

title: Before the Cross
text:
Lord Jesus,
as I stand before your Cross
I see the cost of love without limit.

You stretched out your arms
to embrace the whole world —
the forgotten, the condemned, the afraid.

Help me not to look away.
Help me not to rush past suffering
on my way to somewhere more comfortable.

Give me the courage to stay,
to witness,
and to carry what I can
of the burdens others bear.

Amen.
scripture: Near the cross of Jesus stood his mother. — John 19:25
reflection: What cross am I being invited to carry alongside someone else?

title: A Prayer for Forgiveness
text:
Merciful Father,
we confess that we have sinned against you
and against our neighbour.

We have hoarded when we could have shared.
We have spoken when we should have listened.
We have judged when we should have loved.

In your great mercy,
forgive what we have been,
amend what we are,
and direct what we shall be —
that we may delight in your will
and walk in your ways,
through Jesus Christ our Lord.

Amen.
scripture: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness. — 1 John 1:9
reflection: Is there someone I need to forgive, or someone from whom I need to seek forgiveness?
attribution: Adapted from the Book of Common Prayer
source: General Confession, Book of Common Prayer

title: For the Poorest of the Poor
text:
God of justice and compassion,
we pray for those who live in extreme poverty —
the families who go to sleep hungry,
the children who have never seen a doctor,
the communities without clean water or shelter.

Open our hearts to their cry.
Open our hands to share what we have.
Open our mouths to speak for those
whose voices are ignored.

May our Lenten sacrifices be more than gestures —
may they be the beginning of a more just life.

Through Christ, who became poor for our sake.

Amen.
scripture: Speak up for those who cannot speak for themselves, for the rights of all who are destitute. — Proverbs 31:8
reflection: What small sacrifice can I make today that makes a real difference to someone in need?
attribution: CAFOD Lenten Prayer for the Poor

nextActions.label: Give to the Lent Appeal
nextActions.description: Every gift helps families overcome poverty

title: A Prayer for Light in Darkness
text:
Lord, you are the light of the world,
and in you there is no darkness at all.

Shine into the dark places of our world:
where war destroys families,
where hunger weakens bodies,
where loneliness breaks spirits.

Shine into the dark places of my heart:
where fear holds me back,
where pride keeps me small,
where doubt clouds my vision.

Be my light this Lent.
Be my guide through the valley.
Bring me safely to Easter morning.

Amen.
scripture: The people walking in darkness have seen a great light; on those living in the land of deep darkness a light has dawned. — Isaiah 9:2
reflection: Where is God shining light into my life that I have been reluctant to see?

nextActions.label: Receive weekly reflections
nextActions.description: CAFOD's Friday gospel reflection by email

title: For Refugees and the Displaced
text:
God of all peoples,
we pray for those who have been forced to flee —
from war, from persecution, from disaster.

We remember the families sleeping in camps,
the children who cannot go to school,
the parents who fear for tomorrow.

Jesus, you were a refugee in Egypt.
You know what it means to have no home.
Teach us to welcome the stranger,
to open doors where others build walls,
and to see in every refugee
the face of Christ.

Amen.
scripture: For I was hungry and you gave me something to eat, I was thirsty and you gave me something to drink, I was a stranger and you invited me in. — Matthew 25:35
reflection: How can I be more welcoming to those who are new or displaced in my community?
source: Based on Matthew 25 and Catholic Social Teaching on Migration

title: For the Care of Creation
text:
Loving Creator,
you looked upon all you had made and called it good.
Forgive us for the harm we have done to your creation.

The forests burn. The oceans choke.
The poorest communities bear the heaviest cost
of a crisis they did not cause.

Grant us wisdom to live differently —
to consume less, to share more,
to treat the earth not as a resource to exploit
but as a gift to cherish.

May we hear the cry of the earth
and the cry of the poor as one.

Amen.
scripture: The earth is the Lord's, and everything in it, the world, and all who live in it. — Psalm 24:1
reflection: What is one change I can make this week to live more gently on the earth?

nextActions.label: Join the climate campaign
nextActions.description: Call for climate justice with CAFOD

title: A Prayer for Simplicity
text:
Lord, in a world of excess,
teach me the grace of enough.

Free me from the restless need for more —
more possessions, more approval, more control.

Help me to travel light through this Lent,
carrying only what I need:
your love, your word, and the companionship
of those who walk beside me.

Let me find abundance in simplicity
and richness in giving things away.

Through Christ, who had nowhere to lay his head.

Amen.
scripture: Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moths and vermin destroy. But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven. — Matthew 6:19–20
reflection: What possession or habit is cluttering my relationship with God?

nextActions.label: Take the Live Simply pledge
nextActions.description: Live simply, sustainably, and in solidarity

title: A Prayer of Solidarity
text:
God of all nations,
we are one human family,
bound together by your love.

When our brothers and sisters in distant lands suffer,
we suffer too — even when we do not feel it.
When they rejoice, their joy is ours to share.

Break through the barriers of distance and difference
that allow us to forget we belong to each other.

Make us true neighbours —
not by geography, but by grace.

Through Christ, who crossed every boundary to reach us.

Amen.
scripture: There is neither Jew nor Gentile, neither slave nor free, nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus. — Galatians 3:28
reflection: Who is my neighbour that I have been slow to notice?

nextActions.label: Stand in solidarity
nextActions.description: Support CAFOD's work across the world

title: A Prayer for Healing
text:
Lord Jesus, healer of the sick,
we bring before you all who suffer in body, mind, or spirit.

Touch those who are ill with your gentle hand.
Comfort those who grieve with your presence.
Strengthen those who care for others
when their own strength is failing.

Where medicine cannot reach,
let your love be enough.
Where words cannot comfort,
let your silence speak peace.

We pray especially for communities
without access to healthcare —
where distance, poverty, or conflict
stands between the sick and the help they need.

Amen.
scripture: He heals the broken-hearted and binds up their wounds. — Psalm 147:3
reflection: Who needs my prayer or presence in their suffering today?

title: A Prayer for Courage
text:
God of the prophets,
you gave courage to those who spoke truth to power —
to Moses before Pharaoh,
to Esther before the king,
to Jesus before Pilate.

Give me courage, too.

Courage to speak up when injustice is present.
Courage to act when action is costly.
Courage to love when love is risky.
Courage to hope when the world says it is foolish.

I may not change the world today,
but let me not be silent.

Amen.
scripture: Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go. — Joshua 1:9
reflection: What truth am I being called to speak, even if it is uncomfortable?

nextActions.label: Write to your MP
nextActions.description: Speak up for the world's most vulnerable

title: For the Children of the World
text:
Loving God, protector of the young,
we lift up to you every child in danger:

Children who are hungry.
Children who are frightened.
Children who work when they should play.
Children who have lost their parents.
Children who have never known safety.

You said: 'Let the little children come to me.'
Help us to be your hands —
to feed, to shelter, to educate,
and to fight for a world where every child
can grow up free and loved.

Amen.
scripture: Jesus said, 'Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these.' — Matthew 19:14
reflection: How can I help create a safer, more just world for children?

nextActions.label: Protect children worldwide
nextActions.description: Give to CAFOD's work with families

title: A Lenten Prayer of Gratitude
text:
Lord, even in this season of sacrifice,
I come to you first with thanks.

Thank you for the breath in my lungs.
Thank you for the people who love me.
Thank you for the food on my table
and the roof over my head.

Help me to hold these gifts lightly,
knowing they are not mine to keep
but yours to share.

May my gratitude overflow into generosity,
my abundance into justice,
my prayer into action.

Amen.
scripture: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his love endures for ever. — Psalm 107:1
reflection: What am I most grateful for today, and how can I share that gift?

title: A Prayer for Patience
text:
Lord, teach me to wait.

In a world that demands everything now,
give me the patience to sit with uncertainty,
to endure difficulty without rushing to escape,
and to trust your timing even when I cannot see the plan.

Help me to be patient with others —
especially those who frustrate me,
challenge me, or simply move at a different pace.

Help me to be patient with myself —
with my failures, my slow growth,
my recurring weaknesses.

And help me to wait on you, Lord,
knowing that those who wait
shall renew their strength.

Amen.
scripture: But those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary, they will walk and not be faint. — Isaiah 40:31
reflection: Where am I being invited to practise patience today?

title: He Is Risen!
text:
Alleluia! Christ is risen!

God of life, on this glorious day
you rolled away the stone
and brought your Son from death to life.

Fill us with Easter joy —
not a fleeting happiness,
but the deep, unshakeable knowledge
that love is stronger than death,
hope is stronger than despair,
and light will always overcome the darkness.

Send us out as witnesses to the resurrection,
carrying your good news to a world in need.

Alleluia! He is risen indeed!

Amen.
scripture: He is not here; he has risen, just as he said. Come and see the place where he lay. — Matthew 28:6
reflection: Where do I see signs of resurrection — new life breaking through — in my world today?
source: Based on the Easter Vigil Liturgy

nextActions.label: Easter reflections
nextActions.description: Daily readings for the Easter season

title: A Prayer of Easter Hope
text:
God of all hope,
you are the one who makes all things new.

In the risen Christ we see your promise:
that no suffering is the final word,
that no injustice lasts for ever,
that no grave can hold your love.

Plant this Easter hope deep within us.
Let it grow even when the soil seems dry.
Let it bloom even in the harshest conditions.

And may our hope be contagious —
spreading from heart to heart,
community to community,
until the whole world knows
that death has been defeated.

Amen.
scripture: I am making everything new! — Revelation 21:5
reflection: What situation in my life needs the hope of Easter most?

title: The Peace of the Risen Christ
text:
Lord Jesus, you stood among your disciples
and said: 'Peace be with you.'

Speak that same peace into our troubled world.
Speak it into war zones and refugee camps.
Speak it into divided communities and broken families.
Speak it into my own anxious heart.

Your peace is not the absence of conflict
but the presence of your love
in the midst of all things.

Grant us that peace —
peace that the world cannot give
and the world cannot take away.

Amen.
scripture: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid. — John 14:27
reflection: Where am I being called to be a channel of Christ's peace?

title: The Road to Emmaus
text:
Lord Jesus, you walked with two disciples
on the road to Emmaus.
They were downcast, confused, grieving —
and they did not recognise you.

How often do I walk that same road?
How often are you beside me
and I do not see you?

Open my eyes, Lord.
Help me to recognise you
in the stranger on the road,
in the breaking of the bread,
in the Scripture that sets my heart on fire.

Stay with me, Lord,
for it is nearly evening
and the day is almost over.

Amen.
scripture: Were not our hearts burning within us while he talked with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us? — Luke 24:32
reflection: When has God been present in my life without me realising it at the time?

title: Prayer for a New Creation
text:
Creator God,
in the resurrection of Jesus
you have begun the renewal of all creation.

Where we see destruction, you see possibility.
Where we see endings, you see beginnings.
Where we see death, you see the seed of new life.

Help us to join in your work of renewal —
to care for the earth, to restore what is broken,
to plant seeds of hope in barren ground.

May we live as Easter people,
always looking for signs of your new creation
breaking through.

Amen.
scripture: Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: the old has gone, the new is here! — 2 Corinthians 5:17
reflection: What is one way I can participate in God's renewal of creation this week?
source: Inspired by Laudato Si' and Easter theology

title: A Prayer of Waiting
text:
Lord, we wait for you.

In this season of Advent,
when the days are short and the nights are long,
we light our candles against the darkness
and trust that you are coming.

Teach us the holy art of waiting —
not passive, but expectant;
not idle, but preparing;
not anxious, but hopeful.

As Mary waited,
as the prophets waited,
as the whole creation groans in anticipation,
we wait for you, Emmanuel —
God with us.

Come, Lord Jesus.

Amen.
scripture: Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: the virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and will call him Immanuel. — Isaiah 7:14
reflection: What am I waiting for, and how can I wait with more trust?
source: Based on the O Antiphons and Isaiah 7

nextActions.label: World Gifts catalogue
nextActions.description: Give a gift that changes lives

title: O Come, O Come, Emmanuel
text:
O come, O come, Emmanuel,
and ransom captive Israel.

Lord, we light this Advent candle
as a sign of your coming light.
In a world still held captive
by injustice, greed, and violence,
we cry out for your deliverance.

Come to the places of deepest darkness.
Come to the communities forgotten by the powerful.
Come to the hearts that have given up hope.

Rejoice! Rejoice! Emmanuel
shall come to thee, O Israel.

Amen.
scripture: The people walking in darkness have seen a great light; on those living in the land of deep darkness a light has dawned. — Isaiah 9:2
reflection: Where in the world does God's light need to break through most urgently?

title: The Magnificat — Mary's Song
text:
My soul magnifies the Lord,
and my spirit rejoices in God my Saviour.

For he has looked with favour
on the lowliness of his servant.
Surely, from now on all generations
will call me blessed.

He has scattered the proud in the thoughts of their hearts.
He has brought down the powerful from their thrones,
and lifted up the lowly.
He has filled the hungry with good things,
and sent the rich away empty.

God of Mary's song,
make us instruments of this holy revolution —
where the hungry are fed,
the proud are humbled,
and the lowly are lifted.

Amen.
scripture: He has filled the hungry with good things but has sent the rich away empty. — Luke 1:53
reflection: How does Mary's radical song challenge my own comfortable faith?

title: Prepare the Way
text:
God of the wilderness,
you sent John the Baptist to cry out:
'Prepare the way of the Lord!'

Prepare the way in our hearts —
clear the clutter of consumerism,
level the mountains of pride,
fill the valleys of despair.

Make straight in us a path
for your Son to enter.

Let this Advent be different.
Let it be honest.
Let it be holy.

Amen.
scripture: A voice of one calling in the wilderness, 'Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for him.' — Mark 1:3
reflection: What needs to be cleared away in my life to make room for Christ this Christmas?

title: For Those With No Room
text:
Lord Jesus, born in a stable
because there was no room at the inn,
be close to all who have no home this night.

Be with those sleeping rough in our cities.
Be with families in temporary shelters.
Be with refugees in overcrowded camps.
Be with children who know no stability.

Forgive us when we say 'no room' —
no room in our budgets,
no room in our neighbourhoods,
no room in our hearts.

Make room in us, Lord,
for the stranger, the outcast, the forgotten.

Amen.
scripture: She wrapped him in cloths and placed him in a manger, because there was no guest room available for them. — Luke 2:7
reflection: Where can I make room for someone who has been excluded?

title: Morning Offering
text:
Lord, I offer you this day —
all that I think, and do, and say.

Unite my work with yours.
Unite my joys with yours.
Unite my struggles with yours.

May every conversation be an act of love.
May every task be done with care.
May every encounter be a moment of grace.

And at the end of this day,
may I look back and see your hand
in everything.

Amen.
scripture: This is the day the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it. — Psalm 118:24
reflection: How can I offer this ordinary day to God in an extraordinary way?
source: Adapted from the Apostleship of Prayer

nextActions.label: Daily prayer emails
nextActions.description: Start each day with CAFOD's reflection

title: An Evening Prayer
text:
Lord, as the day draws to a close,
I bring you everything —
the moments of kindness and the moments of failure,
the conversations that lifted me and those that drained me.

Thank you for walking with me through it all.

Forgive what needs forgiving.
Heal what needs healing.
And grant me peaceful rest,
that I may rise tomorrow
ready to serve you with a fresh heart.

Into your hands, O Lord,
I commend my spirit.

Amen.
scripture: In peace I will lie down and sleep, for you alone, Lord, make me dwell in safety. — Psalm 4:8
reflection: What moment today am I most grateful for? What moment do I wish had gone differently?
source: Based on the Liturgy of the Hours

title: A Prayer of Trust
text:
Lord, I don't know what today will bring,
but I know that you are with me.

When the path is unclear, you are my guide.
When the burden is heavy, you are my strength.
When the future is uncertain, you are my hope.

I place my trust in you —
not because life is easy,
but because you are faithful.

Not because I understand your plan,
but because I know your heart.

Lead me, Lord. I will follow.

Amen.
scripture: Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight. — Proverbs 3:5–6
reflection: What am I trying to control that I need to trust to God?

title: A Prayer for Justice
text:
God of justice,
you hear the cry of the oppressed
and you do not turn away.

We pray for a world where every person is treated with dignity —
where no one goes hungry while others waste food,
where no one is enslaved while others profit,
where no child is denied an education
because of where they were born.

Make us restless until justice is done.
Make us bold enough to name injustice when we see it.
Make us generous enough to pay the cost of change.

Your kingdom come, your will be done,
on earth as it is in heaven.

Amen.
scripture: He has shown you, O mortal, what is good. And what does the Lord require of you? To act justly and to love mercy and to walk humbly with your God. — Micah 6:8
reflection: What injustice have I become too comfortable with?
source: Catholic Social Teaching: Justice and Peace

title: Who Is My Neighbour?
text:
Lord Jesus, when asked 'Who is my neighbour?'
you told the story of a man left for dead
and the stranger who stopped to help.

The priest walked by.
The Levite walked by.
But the Samaritan stopped.

Help me to stop.

When I see need, help me not to cross the road.
When I see suffering, help me not to look away.
When I see injustice, help me not to stay silent.

Make me a neighbour to someone today.

Amen.
scripture: Which of these three do you think was a neighbour to the man who fell into the hands of robbers? The expert in the law replied, 'The one who had mercy on him.' Jesus told him, 'Go and do likewise.' — Luke 10:36–37
reflection: Who has God placed in my path today that I might be tempted to walk past?

title: A Prayer of Thankfulness
text:
Lord, today I choose gratitude.

For the gift of life — thank you.
For the people who love me — thank you.
For the food that nourishes me — thank you.
For the beauty of creation — thank you.
For the faith that sustains me — thank you.

Even for the struggles that shape me,
even for the questions that deepen me,
even for the darkness that teaches me to seek the light —
thank you.

May my life be one long act of thanksgiving.

Amen.
scripture: Give thanks in all circumstances; for this is God's will for you in Christ Jesus. — 1 Thessalonians 5:18
reflection: What is one thing I've been taking for granted that I can give thanks for today?

title: For Workers and the Unemployed
text:
God of all labour,
you placed us in this world to work and to create.

Bless those who work today —
in fields and factories, in hospitals and homes,
in offices and on the streets.

Protect those whose work is dangerous.
Give rest to those whose work is exhausting.
Bring justice to those whose labour is exploited.

And for those who cannot find work —
who send out applications that go unanswered,
who feel invisible and forgotten —
renew their hope and open doors.

May all work serve the common good.

Amen.
scripture: Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord, not for human masters. — Colossians 3:23
reflection: How can I bring more dignity and purpose to my own work today?

title: A Prayer for Wisdom
text:
Lord, grant me wisdom —
not the cleverness of the world,
but the deep knowing that comes from you.

Wisdom to choose what is right when what is easy beckons.
Wisdom to speak words that heal, not wound.
Wisdom to listen before I judge.
Wisdom to act with love, not fear.

You gave Solomon wisdom when he asked for it.
I ask for it too —
not for my own glory,
but to serve you and your people well.

Amen.
scripture: If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all without finding fault, and it will be given to you. — James 1:5
reflection: What decision am I facing that most needs God's wisdom?

title: A Prayer for Families
text:
Loving God,
you placed us in families —
not perfect ones, but real ones.

Bless our families today.
Bless the bonds that hold us together
and heal the wounds that pull us apart.

For families separated by war — reunite them.
For families broken by poverty — sustain them.
For families grieving a loss — comfort them.
For families struggling to communicate — give them patience.

May every home be a place of love,
and every family a reflection of your own.

Amen.
scripture: As for me and my household, we will serve the Lord. — Joshua 24:15
reflection: What can I do today to strengthen the bonds within my own family?

nextActions.label: Support families worldwide
nextActions.description: Help CAFOD protect families in crisis

title: Laudato Si' — Praise Be to You
text:
Praise be to you, my Lord,
through our Sister, Mother Earth,
who sustains and governs us.

All-powerful God,
you are present in the whole universe
and in the smallest of your creatures.

Pour out upon us the power of your love,
that we may protect life and beauty.
Fill us with peace,
that we may live as brothers and sisters,
harming no one.

Touch the hearts of those who look only for gain
at the expense of the poor and the earth.

Help us to hear both the cry of the earth
and the cry of the poor.

Amen.
scripture: The heavens declare the glory of God; the skies proclaim the work of his hands. — Psalm 19:1
reflection: How am I responding to the cry of the earth and the cry of the poor?
source: Adapted from Laudato Si' (2015), paragraphs 246 and closing prayer

title: For Peace in Our World
text:
God of peace,
we pray for our broken world.

Where bombs fall, send your protection.
Where hatred divides, send your reconciliation.
Where violence destroys, send your healing.

Give wisdom to leaders who hold the power of war and peace.
Give courage to peacemakers who risk their lives for others.
Give comfort to civilians caught in conflicts they did not choose.

We long for the day when swords are beaten into ploughshares
and nations learn war no more.

Prince of Peace, hasten that day.

Amen.
scripture: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God. — Matthew 5:9
reflection: Where in my own life can I choose peace over conflict today?
source: Based on Isaiah 2:4 and the Beatitudes

title: A Harvest Prayer
text:
Lord of the harvest,
we thank you for the earth that feeds us,
for the rain that waters the ground,
for the sun that ripens the grain,
and for the hands that gather it in.

We remember that not all harvests are equal.
Some fields lie parched while others flood.
Some farmers reap abundance while others lose everything.

Teach us to share the earth's bounty fairly —
so that all may eat,
all may be nourished,
and none may go hungry
in a world of plenty.

Amen.
scripture: The earth has yielded its harvest; God, our God, has blessed us. — Psalm 67:6
reflection: How can I be more mindful of where my food comes from and who grows it?

nextActions.label: Give to Harvest Fast Day
nextActions.description: Help families grow enough to eat

title: The Canticle of the Sun
text:
Most High, all-powerful, all-good Lord,
all praise is yours, all glory, all honour,
and all blessing.

Be praised, my Lord, through all your creatures,
especially through my lord Brother Sun,
who brings the day; and you give light through him.

Be praised, my Lord, through Sister Moon and the stars;
in the heavens you have made them bright, precious, and beautiful.

Be praised, my Lord, through Sister Water;
she is very useful and humble and precious and pure.

Be praised, my Lord, through our sister Mother Earth,
who feeds us and rules us,
and produces various fruits with coloured flowers and herbs.

Praise and bless my Lord, and give thanks,
and serve him with great humility.

Amen.
scripture: Let everything that has breath praise the Lord. — Psalm 150:6
reflection: Which part of creation am I most drawn to praise God for today?
